title: cordia ecalyculata
intro: Cordia ecalyculata, a species of flowering plant in the bignonia family, is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and bark have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Cordia ecalyculata have been linked to various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Cordia ecalyculata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
